title: Capital Gain Timing and Recognition Planning

requirements_for_qualification.summary: Must strategically time recognition of capital gains across tax years to manage tax brackets, utilize 0% capital gain rate brackets, offset losses, and optimize overall tax liability through multi-year planning
criteria: Analyze capital gain realization timing to manage taxable income and marginal tax brackets across multiple years
2025 long-term capital gain rates: 0% (up to $47,025 single/$94,050 MFJ), 15% (up to $518,900/$583,750), 20% (above)
Hold appreciated assets over 12 months for preferential long-term capital gain rates vs ordinary income rates
Consider net investment income tax (NIIT) 3.8% surtax on investment income above $200,000/$250,000 thresholds
Harvest capital gains in low-income years to utilize 0% rate bracket without triggering tax
Defer gains to future years if expecting lower income or tax rates in those years
Accelerate gains if expecting higher income or tax rates in future years
Coordinate with loss harvesting, charitable donations of appreciated assets, and retirement account distributions

capital_gain_timing_factors.rate_brackets_2025: 0% rate for income in 10-12% ordinary brackets; 15% for most taxpayers; 20% for high earners
capital_gain_timing_factors.holding_period: Must hold over 12 months for long-term rates; 12 months or less taxed as ordinary income
capital_gain_timing_factors.niit_surtax: Additional 3.8% tax on net investment income when MAGI exceeds $200,000/$250,000/$125,000 (S/MFJ/MFS)
capital_gain_timing_factors.bracket_management: Strategic realization keeps taxable income in targeted brackets across multiple years
capital_gain_timing_factors.zero_percent_opportunity: Single filers with income under $47,025 can realize LTCG tax-free up to threshold
capital_gain_timing_factors.loss_offset_coordination: Capital losses offset gains dollar-for-dollar; $3,000 ordinary loss deduction annually

restrictions: Wash sale rule prevents loss recognition if repurchase substantially identical security within 61-day window
Constructive sale rules prevent deferral through short sales, futures, collars on appreciated positions
Must consider state tax implications; many states don't provide preferential capital gain rates
Alternative Minimum Tax (AMT) may affect capital gain tax rates and bracket thresholds
Collectibles, IRC §1202 qualified small business stock, and unrecaptured §1250 gain subject to special rates
Medicare premium income-related monthly adjustment (IRMAA) triggered by large gain recognition
Financial aid calculations (FAFSA) include capital gains potentially reducing aid eligibility
Estate planning consideration: stepped-up basis at death eliminates built-in gains permanently
